Re: Your Reguest for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I -91- 08 0
Dear Mr. Andrus:
You have requested assistance concgrning the ne$, provisions
of the Political 
Reform Act (the uAct') 1 liniting 
gifts and
honoraria. Since you have not naned a specific elected official
on whose behalf you have requested this advice, we provide the
following infornal response to your question''
OUESTTON
Are Pa)rments to a loca1 elected official 
in connection with a
speech giveir, article published, or attendance at any public or
piivate 
- conference, convention, meeting, social event, mea1, or
iike gathering gifis subject to the new gift and honoraria limits
of the Act?
CONCLUSION
Reimbursement for actual travel exPenses and reasonable
subsistence in connection with a speech given, article published,
or attendance at any public or private conference, convention,
rneeting, social event, nea1, or like gathering is exenpted from

are to the Governrnent code unless otherwise indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 8O0 . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916)322'56@


Our Fi■e No. ■-9■-080
Page 2
the $1,ooo linit on gifts and honoraria applicable to local
elected officials.
However, absent sone express exception in the Act, aII public
officials nust disclose the nane and address of each source of
gifts and honoraria of $50 or nore in va1ue, the amount and the
date on which the gi.ft was received, and a general description of
the business activity of the donor. Moreover, a public official
nay not nake, participate in naking or in any way attempt to use
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision
which wilI have a naterial financial effect, distinguishable frorn
its effect on the public aenerally, on any donor of, or any
internediary or agent for a donor of, gifts or honoraria
aggregating $250 or rnore in value provided to, received by, or
promised to the public official within 12 nonths prior to the time
when the decision is nade.
DISCUSSION
Section 89501 provides:
No local etected officeholder shall accept any
honoraria for any speech given, article published,
or attendance at any public or private conference,
convention, meeting, social event, neal, or like
gathering, or any gifts, fron any single source,
which is in excess of one thousand dollars
(91,000), in any calendar year, except
refunbursernent for actual travel expenses and
reasonable subsistence in connection therewith.
Based on this section local elected officers are now subject
to a $1,ooo aggregate linit for gifts and honoraria received from
any single source. However, the statute specifically excludes
fron the $1,ooo linit reimbursenent for actual travel expenses and
reasonable subsistence in connection with a speech given, article
published, or attendance at any public or private conference,
Lonvention, neeting, social event, neaI, or Iike gathering.3
Please note, however, absent some express exception in the
Act, aJ.1 public officials nust disclose the nane and address of
each source of gifts of $50 or nore in value, the anount and the
date on which the gift was received, and a general description of
3 sirr"" the exceptions for travel in section 89506 are
necessarily included within the broader exception in section
89501, we are advising that Section 89506 is not applicable to
loca1 elected officers. This is an issue which rnay be the subject
of future Cororoission regulation.

Page 3
the business activity of the donor.l
public officials nay not make, participate in rnaking or in any way
attenpt to use his or her officlal position to influence a
governmental decision which wiII have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on any
donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or
gifts aggregating S250 or nore in value provided to, received by,
or pronised to the public official within 12 nonths prior to the
tine when the decision is rnade. (Sections 87100, e'E seo.)
If you have any further guestions regarding this natter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-59OL,)
(Section a7207. ) Moreover,
Sincerely,

schedule established by the Conraission or shalI be reported as a
gift. 
(Regulation 18728(b).) If it is clear from all of the
iurrounding circumstances that the servi'ces provided to the source
of the payment was equal or greater consideration than the palnnent
received,-the honorarium may be disclosed as incone. However, the
pubJ.ic official 
has the burden of provinq that the consideration
was of equal or greater value, unless the public official 
is a
defendant in a criroinal action. 
(Regulation 18728(c).)
' 
copies of commission regnrlations and opinions are available in
rnany 1aw libraries.

cost of 10C per page.
